purpose: Define the Phase 6B commerce schema model contract before Prisma implementation, without authorizing business capability behavior.

common_model_policy.tenant_scope: Every Phase 6B table is tenant scoped through organization_id unless a later accepted ADR says otherwise.
common_model_policy.organization_relation: Every model must relate to Organization using organization_id.
common_model_policy.audit_policy: Entity registry metadata must mark audit_required=true for every Phase 6B model.
common_model_policy.rls_policy: Entity registry metadata must mark tenant_scoped=true, organization_id_required=true, and rls_required=true for every Phase 6B model.
common_model_policy.capability_behavior_policy: No pricing engine, payment gateway behavior, communication send behavior, inventory movement logic, GL posting logic, payroll calculation, or UI workflow is authorized by this contract.
common_model_policy.json_payload_policy: payload_json and evidence_json fields are baseline capture surfaces; future capability tickets may harden typed fields only after source-grounded implementation review.

component_model_groups.owned_data_summary: Products, categories, product media, product status, and product history.
Product price histories, effective dates, package and bundle definitions, and pricing configuration extensions controlled through Product service.
Stock locations, lots/serials, stock levels, stock movements, and evidence references.
Lead sources, intake mappings, consent basis, and intake evidence.
Duplicate match candidates, merge records, and duplicate decisions.
Pipeline stages, activities, notes, timeline entries, and stage history.
Templates, sequence enrollments, conversation metadata, and send-attempt evidence with gateway enforcement for send attempts.
Lead scores, follow-up tasks, reporting definitions, and compliance views.
Invoices, invoice lines, receivables, payment terms, and credit/debit notes.
Payments, allocations, receipts, top-ups, provider references, callbacks, and reconciliation candidates.
Vendors, expenses, purchase orders, approvals, receipts, and purchase evidence.
Chart of accounts, journal entries, accounting periods, and tax mappings.
Bank accounts, imported transactions, matches, and reconciliation statements.
Payees, payroll batches, payout records, deductions, and allowance formulas.
Tenant billing views, balance displays, budget caps, receipts, and service spend views.

validation_expectations: P6B-SCHEMA-001 review must verify every v17 execution seed appears exactly once in seed_to_model_decision_group_mappings.
P6B-SCHEMA-001 review must verify every proposed model has at least one seed in model_seed_coverage.
P6B-SCHEMA-002 must not start until this contract is explicitly accepted by human review.
P6B-SCHEMA-002 must add only additive Phase 6B schema baseline models and migration SQL.
P6B-SCHEMA-003 must align contracts/manifests/NestJS scaffold metadata without implementing business behavior.
P6B-SCHEMA-004 must regenerate v18 from live repo truth and promote only genuinely grounded capability tickets.
correction_reason: Phase 6B recovery review found that the prior contract declared 103 seed coverage but did not include explicit seed-to-model-decision-group mappings. This repair adds 103/103 mappings and corrects stale component seed counts from v17.

seed_to_model_decision_group_mappings.mapping_basis: Mapped from v17 execution_seed_matrix seed_id/source_component_id plus P6B-SCHEMA-001 proposed model group; this is schema-control planning evidence only and does not authorize capability behavior.
